1-to-2.title: Deploy Multi-Factor Authentication
1-to-2.description: Implement phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/passkeys) for all user accounts, prioritizing privileged users and remote access.

1-to-2.title: Establish Identity Provider (IdP)
1-to-2.description: Deploy a centralized IdP to consolidate authentication, enabling SSO and consistent policy enforcement across applications.

1-to-2.title: Implement Basic PAM Controls
1-to-2.description: Create named privileged accounts, eliminate shared admin credentials, and enforce session recording for all privileged access.

2-to-3.title: Deploy Privileged Access Management (PAM)
2-to-3.description: Implement a PAM solution with approval workflows, session recording, and password vaulting for all privileged accounts.

2-to-3.title: Implement Identity Governance (IGA)
2-to-3.description: Deploy an IGA platform to automate joiner-mover-leaver workflows and enforce access certification campaigns quarterly.

2-to-3.title: Enforce Universal SSO
2-to-3.description: Federate all business applications (SaaS and internal) to the central IdP; eliminate legacy authentication protocols (NTLM, basic auth).

2-to-3.title: Deploy UEBA for Identity Anomaly Detection
2-to-3.description: Integrate identity telemetry with SIEM/UEBA to baseline normal user behavior and alert on deviations.

3-to-4.title: Implement Just-in-Time Privileged Access
3-to-4.description: Eliminate standing privileges — require on-demand privilege elevation with automated expiration and full audit trails.

3-to-4.title: Deploy Workload Identity Federation
3-to-4.description: Replace service account passwords and static API keys with OIDC/SPIFFE-based short-lived workload identities.

3-to-4.title: Enable Continuous Risk-Adaptive Authentication
3-to-4.description: Implement step-up authentication triggered by risk signals (new device, unusual location, anomalous behavior) with automated access suspension.

1-to-2.title: Deploy Mobile Device Management (MDM)
1-to-2.description: Enroll all corporate devices in an MDM/UEM platform to enforce baseline configuration policies and enable remote wipe.

1-to-2.title: Establish Device Inventory
1-to-2.description: Implement automated asset discovery to build and maintain a real-time inventory of all endpoints.

1-to-2.title: Deploy EDR on All Endpoints
1-to-2.description: Replace legacy antivirus with EDR coverage across all managed endpoints for behavioral detection and response capability.

2-to-3.title: Enforce Device Compliance at Access Time
2-to-3.description: Integrate MDM compliance signals with identity provider to gate access based on real-time device health status.

2-to-3.title: Implement PKI-based Device Certificates
2-to-3.description: Deploy device certificates via MDM for cryptographic device authentication — replace IP/hostname-based trust.

2-to-3.title: Enforce Automated Patch Management
2-to-3.description: Automate OS and application patching with compliance reporting; block access for devices with critical unpatched vulnerabilities.

2-to-3.title: Implement BYOD Isolation Policy
2-to-3.description: Restrict unmanaged devices to browser-based access or VDI — no corporate data persisted on unmanaged endpoints.

3-to-4.title: Implement Hardware-Backed Device Attestation
3-to-4.description: Leverage TPM/Secure Enclave for hardware-rooted device identity, preventing spoofed device certificates.

3-to-4.title: Integrate EDR Signals into ZT Policy Engine
3-to-4.description: Feed EDR risk scores into the policy decision point — devices under active investigation are automatically quarantined.

1-to-2.title: Implement Network Segmentation
1-to-2.description: Segment the network into security zones (production, development, corporate, DMZ) using VLANs and firewall rules.

1-to-2.title: Deploy ZTNA for Remote Access
1-to-2.description: Replace VPN with a ZTNA solution to grant application-level remote access without exposing the network.

1-to-2.title: Implement DNS Filtering
1-to-2.description: Deploy cloud-based DNS security to block malware callbacks, phishing domains, and C2 communication.

2-to-3.title: Deploy Application-Aware Micro-Segmentation
2-to-3.description: Implement micro-segmentation policies at the workload level, restricting lateral movement to explicitly permitted flows only.

2-to-3.title: Enable Internal Traffic Inspection
2-to-3.description: Deploy IDS/IPS on internal network segments to detect lateral movement and east-west attack patterns.

2-to-3.title: Implement TLS Inspection
2-to-3.description: Deploy SSL/TLS inspection at perimeter and cloud egress points to prevent threats from hiding in encrypted traffic.

3-to-4.title: Adopt SSE/SASE Architecture
3-to-4.description: Consolidate network security on a cloud-delivered SSE/SASE platform (SWG, CASB, ZTNA) with unified policy enforcement.

3-to-4.title: Integrate DNS Telemetry into ZT Policy
3-to-4.description: Feed DNS anomaly signals (DGA detection, unexpected resolvers) into the SIEM and ZT policy engine.

1-to-2.title: Deploy Application Proxy / ZTNA for Internal Apps
1-to-2.description: Publish internal applications via an application proxy or ZTNA broker — remove direct network access to application servers.

1-to-2.title: Implement Centralized API Gateway
1-to-2.description: Route all API traffic through a managed API gateway with authentication, rate limiting, and logging.

1-to-2.title: Centralize Secrets Management
1-to-2.description: Deploy a secrets vault (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, cloud-native KMS) to eliminate hardcoded credentials across applications.

2-to-3.title: Implement Workload Identity for Cloud Resources
2-to-3.description: Replace static IAM keys with managed identities, instance profiles, or IRSA across all cloud workloads.

2-to-3.title: Integrate Security Scanning into CI/CD
2-to-3.description: Add SAST, DAST, SCA, and container scanning to CI/CD pipelines with policy gates that fail builds on critical findings.

2-to-3.title: Deploy CASB for SaaS Governance
2-to-3.description: Implement a CASB to discover shadow SaaS, enforce approved-app policies, and apply DLP controls on SaaS data.

3-to-4.title: Implement SPIFFE/SPIRE for Workload Identity
3-to-4.description: Deploy a SPIFFE/SPIRE-based workload identity fabric for cryptographic service-to-service authentication across all environments.

3-to-4.title: Enable Runtime Application Self-Protection (RASP)
3-to-4.description: Instrument applications with RASP or behavioral analytics to detect and block application-layer attacks in real time.

3-to-4.title: Implement Dynamic Secrets with Automated Rotation
3-to-4.description: Eliminate all long-lived application credentials — shift to dynamic, short-lived secrets with automated lifecycle management.

1-to-2.title: Implement Data Classification Policy
1-to-2.description: Define a data classification taxonomy (e.g., Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) and begin automated labeling of critical data stores.

1-to-2.title: Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest and in Transit
1-to-2.description: Enable encryption for all databases, file stores, and backups containing sensitive data; enforce TLS 1.2+ for all data in transit.

1-to-2.title: Enable Data Access Logging
1-to-2.description: Enable audit logging on all databases and file systems containing sensitive data with centralized log aggregation.

2-to-3.title: Deploy Data Loss Prevention (DLP)
2-to-3.description: Implement DLP controls across email, endpoints, and cloud storage using classification labels to enforce data handling policies.

2-to-3.title: Implement Attribute-Based Access Control for Data
2-to-3.description: Migrate from broad RBAC to fine-grained ABAC for sensitive data access, driven by data classification and user attributes.

2-to-3.title: Deploy DSPM for Cloud Data Discovery
2-to-3.description: Implement Cloud Data Security Posture Management to discover and classify sensitive data across cloud storage and databases.

3-to-4.title: Implement Customer-Managed Encryption Keys
3-to-4.description: Deploy CMEK/BYOK with HSM for critical data assets — retain cryptographic control over your most sensitive data.

3-to-4.title: Enable AI-Driven Data Classification
3-to-4.description: Upgrade to ML-based classification for real-time discovery and labeling of new sensitive data as it is created or ingested.

3-to-4.title: Implement Just-in-Time Data Access
3-to-4.description: Require explicit, time-limited access requests for sensitive data — no standing read access to production data stores.

1-to-2.title: Deploy a Centralized SIEM
1-to-2.description: Implement a cloud-native or on-prem SIEM with log ingestion from identity, endpoint, network, and application sources.

1-to-2.title: Implement Compliance Monitoring
1-to-2.description: Deploy automated compliance scanning tools aligned to selected frameworks, replacing manual audit processes.

2-to-3.title: Expand SIEM Coverage Across All ZT Pillars
2-to-3.description: Ensure all six ZT pillars contribute telemetry to the SIEM — close gaps in device, data, and application log ingestion.

2-to-3.title: Deploy SOAR Playbooks for Common Threats
2-to-3.description: Automate response to top 10 alert types (phishing, brute force, lateral movement) using SOAR playbooks.

2-to-3.title: Integrate Threat Intelligence Feeds
2-to-3.description: Enrich SIEM detections with curated threat intelligence to improve signal fidelity and reduce false positives.

3-to-4.title: Automate ZT Policy Enforcement from SIEM Detections
3-to-4.description: Integrate SOAR with the ZT policy engine — SIEM detections automatically trigger access revocation, device quarantine, or step-up auth.

3-to-4.title: Implement Continuous Compliance as Code
3-to-4.description: Encode compliance requirements as policy-as-code, enabling continuous attestation and automated evidence collection for all frameworks.
